_doc: AUTHORED sidecar: the human meaning of each gate flag (group:index) used by BOTH the enemy (spawn_control) and item (item_control) layers. This is the ONLY hand-edited input to the trigger-summary layer; everything else in room-data.json's summary/logic/_flag_glossary is DERIVED from the decoded control blocks + these labels by scripts/trigger_decode.py. 'label' is a short event-noun used verbatim in summaries; 'event_room' is the room whose script sets the flag (informational; the primary 0x25 setter). Adding a new gated room: run `scripts/trigger_decode.py --check`; if it reports an unlabeled flag, add it here.
_basis: Covers BOTH the enemy gates (spawn_control) and the item gates (item_control). Enemy group-0 flags 0:9/0:15/0:35/0:58/0:63 are CE/doc/notepad-confirmed (docs/dc1/ROOM-SPAWN-CONDITIONS.md). The rest are named from the static self-latch pattern (the room's own event sub sets the flag that gates its placement) plus the 0x25 setter trace; narrative names are INFERRED, not byte-proven. The Backup-Generator cluster (0:44/0:45/0:47/0:51) is corroborated at the area level by the beta-list notepad (the 'Passaggio Gen. di Riserva' dinosaur whose fate gates later scenes). Flags with no 0x25 setter (e.g. 0:0/0:1/0:5/0:6 and the group-7 item flags) are engine/native/pickup-set; their narrative is unverified. ITEM flag banks: group 7 = engine/pickup-set item-placement flags (no script setter; via 0x26), group 11 = key/item-held (via the 0x52 test op), group 2 = scenario-segment state, plus puzzle-state group-0 flags. Item gate STRUCTURE is byte-decoded (confirmed); the flag NAMES are inferred at the same grade as the enemy ones. cont.61 additions (STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.61 §A–§C): 0:57 (only corpus setter = 0114 sub12) and 0:34/0:38 (010A sub8/sub15) are byte-cited event flags; 2:9/2:10 are the NATIVE screen success/fail protocol (SetFlag at 0x4B182A and the keypad comparators, no script setter); 0:22/0:24 RESOLVED by the cont.62 fence state-driver decode (STATIC-SCD-RE.md cont.62): 0:22 = the 0106 sub8 first-visit latch that ARMS the 1F keypad-unlock events (0102 sub6 -> 0:241; 0107 sub8 zone -> 0:240/243/244, which also needs 7:85 = the 0202 DDK Input Disc N pickup taken); 0:24 = the 0106 sub16 self-latch, NOT a fence unlock (its 0:249 arm is bonus-mode-only, gated on 0:1 which has no campaign writer). Fence flags 0:12/13/32/36/78/79/98/99/112/147/149/173/177 (0=beams active/blocking) are polled per-frame by the shared type-4 screen handler 0x4B0E10; keypad enables 0:240-253 are read natively by the toggle FSM 0x44B90D (subtype-11 AOT rec+0x1F); all byte-cited, no CE needed.
